title: Boat Rental Dolphin Watching in Puerto Banus: A 2026 Guide
html:
Puerto Banus, the glamorous marina on the Costa del Sol, is one of the best places in Europe to see bottlenose dolphins in the wild. A typical half-day dolphin-watching charter on the Mediterranean Sea costs between €600 and €1,500, and you can book a skippered trip through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace that connects you with verified boat owners.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus for dolphin watching?

Prices start from around €600 for a half-day shared tour and go up to €2,500 for a full-day private yacht charter. The final cost depends on the boat size, duration, and whether you choose a crewed or bareboat option. For the best value, consider a small motorboat for up to eight guests from €800 for four hours. Catamaran charters, which offer more stability and space, typically cost from €1,400 for a full day. A crewed luxury yacht with a skipper and host can reach €2,500. To You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

Many visitors combine a dolphin-watching trip with a stop at the exclusive Marbella Club or a swim off the coast of Cabopino. The stretch of water from Puerto Banus to the Bay of Gibraltar is a known dolphin habitat, and pods of common and striped dolphins are frequently sighted within 15 nautical miles of the marina. A typical charter lasts three to four hours and covers around 20 nautical miles. One option worth a look is the Maiora 26m for hire.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is the main departure point, with dozens of charter companies operating from its well-equipped marina. Other nearby marinas include Puerto José Banus (the main harbour), Puerto de la Duquesa to the west, and Puerto de Marbella to the east. The bay of Marbella, the Costa del Sol coastline, and the waters off Estepona are all popular cruising grounds known for dolphin activity. For a longer trip, head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar where pilot whales and even orcas are sometimes spotted during summer. You can also see more at check availability.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Skipper-led motorboat (8-30ft), ideal for small groups seeking a private tour; up to 8 guests; from €800 (half-day).
  2. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut, Sunseeker), perfect for a premium experience with a spacious flybridge; up to 12 guests; from €1,800 (full-day).
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon, Fountaine Pajot), stable and comfortable with plenty of deck space; up to 12 guests; from €1,300 (full-day).
  4. Rib or rigid inflatable boat, fast and exciting for adventure seekers; up to 6 guests; from €500 (half-day).
  5. Sailing yacht, traditional and quieter, allowing closer wildlife encounters; up to 6 guests; from €900 (half-day).

When choosing a boat, consider the number of guests and desired comfort level. A luxury motor yacht such as the Maiora 26m for hire is a popular choice for larger groups, offering ample space and amenities. This 26-metre vessel is based at Puerto Banus and can accommodate up to 20 guests for a dolphin-watching trip. To see how booking works, visit the booking FAQ.

Season and licence requirements for boating in Puerto Banus

Dolphin sightings are most reliable from March to October, with peak season in July and August. The local wind, known as the Levante, can bring choppy conditions, so morning departures are often calmer. If you plan to self-drive, note that most motorboats over 15hp require a valid boating licence. However, all charters booked through BoatHire24 include a licensed skipper, so you do not need any prior experience. For exact requirements, the operator confirms the necessary qualifications.

Tip: book a morning trip from Puerto Banus when the sea is calmest and dolphins are most active. The light is also better for photography.

At a glance

  • Price band: €600, €2,500 (half-day/full-day)
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 3, 4 hours
  • Capacity: up to 20 guests on larger yachts
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: not required on skippered charters

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I see dolphins on a boat trip from Puerto Banus?

Yes, the Mediterranean waters off Puerto Banus are home to several dolphin species, and sighting rates on dedicated tours exceed 85 per cent during the summer months.

How long is a typical dolphin-watching charter?

Most charters last between three and four hours, covering approximately 20 nautical miles from the marina to the prime dolphin habitats.

What is included in the price of a boat rental in Puerto Banus?

A typical private charter includes the boat, skipper, fuel, and basic safety equipment; drinks and food are often provided on premium yachts but always confirm with the operator.

For detailed booking information, including what to expect and how to cancel, consult the booking FAQ on the BoatHire24 website before reserving your trip.

To explore verified boat options and secure your dolphin-watching adventure, check availability on BoatHire24. If you own a vessel in Puerto Banus, you can list your boat and earn income by hosting guests.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
